page: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is an x86_64 server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 8 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz, with 8 physical cores and 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a 480 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. There are no dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU testing, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all scoring in the top 10%. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the average tier, as evidenced by stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. Large-block memory write bandwidth is recorded at 41.17 GB/sec. For language model workloads, text generation with a small 135M model achieves top-tier speeds of 372.5 tokens/sec, whereas prompt processing for a medium 7B model drops to the bottom 25% tier at 31.93 tokens/sec.

The shared CPU allocation presents a resource-sharing tradeoff, which may affect consistency under sustained multi-core loads, aligning with its average multi-core benchmark results. However, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated physical CPU cores. This server is suited for database operations, memory-cached web serving, and lightweight single-threaded processing tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is an x86_64 virtual server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD. It features a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though its multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential performance variability under sustained multi-threaded workloads. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and lightweight text processing tasks that benefit from a larger memory footprint without requiring dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is a VX1 Cloud Compute server with 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-8c-64g-480s is a virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

Bundled 480 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities
Suited for database operations and memory-cached web serving
